title: carex filipes subsp. oligostachys
intro: Carex filipes subsp. oligostachys, also known as the hairy sedg or hairy bog sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carex filipes subsp. oligostachys include its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, reduce fever, and support respiratory health. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
